page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring custom AWS Graviton processors built on the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 architecture. It delivers 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz with a single thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, featuring a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Networking is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory performance, with a Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25% and a cached read-only memory bandwidth of 386.23 GB/sec. CPU performance is generally moderate; stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, along with Geekbench compiling and text processing, fall in the middle 50% average. However, the instance shows limitations in specific workloads, with weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and poor performance in CPU encryption tests and medium-model LLM inference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7gd.xlarge offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive applications by leveraging energy-efficient ARM architecture and high-density memory without the overhead of hyperthreading. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage makes it highly suitable for workloads requiring fast temporary storage. Recommended workloads include memory-intensive databases, caching layers such as Redis, static web serving, and general-purpose memory-optimized tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by 4 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong memory capabilities, with Passmark memory scores in the top 25%, alongside average CPU and database performance. However, it exhibits weaker results in cryptographic and multi-threaded decompression tasks. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, making it well-suited for caching layers like Redis, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 4 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong memory performance for databases and caching.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong memory bandwidth for database workloads.

Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, delivering 8.0 GB per core
Includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store
Strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% of Passmark benchmarks
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and static web serving
